message: bd-lff4p.4.8 optimize ftui-tty burst input path

files.patch:
@@ -1366,7 +1366,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","title":"Implement: WASM step-based Program runner (no blocking poll, no threads)","description":"Goal
- Implement a deterministic, JS-driven runtime loop for WASM.

Requirements
- Inputs are delivered from JS (keyboard/mouse/resize/ticks).
- Rendering produces either ftui-render Buffer diffs or FrankenTerm patches.
- No `std::thread` assumptions.

Acceptance Criteria
- Can drive an ftui Model with: init -> input -> view -> present cycles.
- Deterministic record/replay possible.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireRidge","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:17.338911499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:51.681740567Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:30.433421161Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:17.338911499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:17.338911499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:17.338911499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1015,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SapphireRidge claiming. Both blockers (bd-lff4p.3.2 and bd-lff4p.3.3) are closed. Starting implementation of WASM step-based Program runner in ftui-web.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:36:26Z"},{"id":1016,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SapphireRidge: Implementation complete (commit 8bedf73e). StepProgram in ftui-web provides host-driven, non-blocking Model runner with: init/step lifecycle, DeterministicClock integration, WebBackend buffer capture, BufferDiff computation, tick handling, synchronous task execution. 22 new tests all passing. Also fixed BufferDiff::compute error in ftui-runtime WasmRunner. All quality gates green (fmt, clippy, tests, wasm32 compilation).","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:29Z"},{"id":1017,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"WASM step-based runner complete (8bedf73e). Two complementary implementations: (1) ftui-runtime/wasm_runner.rs - platform-agnostic WasmRunner<M> with step/render/push_event API, Buffer+BufferDiff output, 16 tests; (2) ftui-web/step_program.rs - web-specific StepProgram<M> using WebBackend with deterministic clock + presenter integration, 22 tests. Both compile for wasm32-unknown-unknown. Acceptance criteria met: init->input->view->present cycles work, deterministic record/replay proven via tests.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.5","title":"Integrate: ftui-render Buffer -> FrankenTerm patch feed","description":"Goal
- Connect ftui-render output to FrankenTerm so the browser renderer can present ftui frames efficiently.

Options
- Convert ftui-render Buffer diffs into FrankenTerm patch representation.
- Or define a shared patch protocol used by both.

Acceptance Criteria
- No full-frame copies on steady-state updates.
- End-to-end demo updates are visibly smooth.

Three functions: cell_from_render (Cell→CellData), diff_to_patches (BufferDiff→Vec<CellPatch> with adjacent coalescing), full_buffer_patch (full buffer). 10 tests, all green. Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:51:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","title":"Demo: run ftui-demo-showcase in browser via ftui-web + FrankenTerm","description":"Goal
- Run `ftui-demo-showcase` fully client-side in the browser via `ftui-web` + `frankenterm-web` (no xterm.js).

Scope
- Boot, render, navigate, resize, and respond to keyboard + mouse with low latency.
- Any OS-dependent features must either:
  - have explicit web implementations, OR
  - be explicitly disabled with user-visible UX (no silent partial failure).
- Perf + stability: no leaks, no jank, and predictable frame-time under typical demo workloads.

Testing + Logging (Non-negotiable)
- Create a browser "demo sweep" analogous to `tests/e2e/scripts/test_demo_showcase_sweep.sh`:
  - emits detailed JSONL (screen_id, seed, size, DPR, timings, frame/patch hashes)
  - failures emit minimal repro traces that can be replayed
- Determinism: recorded sessions replay to identical hashes on the same build.

Acceptance Criteria
- A single web page can run the demo for 10+ minutes without runaway memory or degraded responsiveness.
- JSONL logs validate against the shared schema and are rich enough to debug mismatches.
- Seed/time-freeze determinism is preserved end-to-end.

-{"id":"bd-lff4p.3.7","title":"Determinism: record/replay web sessions (input + resize + ticks)","description":"Goal
- Ensure we can deterministically reproduce bugs and perf issues in the browser.

Requirements
- Record: input events, resize events, tick deltas, and any nondeterminism sources.
- Replay: drive ftui-web runner to exact same rendered output checksums.

Acceptance Criteria
- A recorded session replays to identical frame checksums on same build.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4","title":"ftui-native backend: replace Crossterm (Unix-first)","description":"Scope
- Replace Crossterm with a first-party backend: raw mode, alt-screen, mouse, bracketed paste, focus events, kitty keyboard, resize, and robust cleanup.
- Drive input via ftui-core InputParser on raw bytes (no Crossterm event translation).

Deliverables
- New backend crate (name TBD) and integration into ftui-core/ftui-runtime.
- PTY + panic-cleanup tests; perf improvements via fewer syscalls + tighter buffering.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:31.341662263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:31.341662263Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:31.341662263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1377,7 +1377,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","title":"Integrate: swap ftui runtime to new backend (native path)","description":"Goal
- Wire the new native backend into `ftui-runtime` and remove all direct Crossterm coupling from the runtime hot path.

Non-Negotiable Compatibility
- Behavior must match current demo semantics (input, resize, mouse, paste, focus, kitty keyboard, alt-screen/inline rules).
- One-writer rule and terminal cleanup semantics must remain correct under normal exit and panic/unwind.

Testing + Logging
- Run the existing PTY E2E suite (`tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh` or curated subsets) against the new backend.
- E2E logs must validate against `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json` and include backend identity + capability profile.
- Add/extend tests as needed to cover any behavior differences discovered during migration.

Acceptance Criteria
- `ftui-demo-showcase` runs on the new backend with identical user-visible behavior.
- E2E suite is green and produces detailed JSONL logs usable for regression triage.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:58:27.329741487Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:58:27.329720478Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1009,"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PinkElk starting integration: swapping CrosstermEventSource for TtyEventSource in runtime","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:49:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.6","title":"Remove: Crossterm dependency from ftui-core and ftui-runtime","description":"Goal
- Delete Crossterm from the core build graph and own the terminal backend end-to-end.

Acceptance Criteria
- `rg crossterm` shows no production dependencies (tests may still reference during migration, but should be deleted once validated).
- Workspace builds and tests pass.

- Tests validate cleanup on panic/unwind paths.

- Measured present-time reductions (p95) in demo showcase.
- Changes follow Extreme Optimization loop (one lever, isomorphism proof).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5","title":"FrankenTerm and ftui-web QA: golden traces, fuzz, extreme optimization","description":"Scope
- Correctness and performance engineering for the entire FrankenTerm + ftui-web stack (core/web/native/remote).

Deliverables
- Golden systems
  - Versioned trace format + replay engine + checksum gates
  - Renderer-level goldens (frame/pixel hashes) for web
- Test stack
  - Proptest invariants + fuzz targets for parsers/state machines
  - Native PTY E2E scripts with detailed JSONL logging
  - Web/remote E2E scripts with deterministic traces + JSONL
- CI + Perf gates
  - wasm build checks
  - time-bounded fuzz/proptest smoke
  - trace replay gates
  - benchmark/perf regression thresholds (with optional statistical guards)

Operating Principle
- Every optimization is single-lever, measured, and backed by isomorphism proof + golden verification.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","title":"Design: golden trace format for terminal sessions (native + web)","description":"Goal
- Define a stable, versioned trace format we can use to reproduce bugs and gate regressions across core/web/native/remote.

Format Requirements
- Inputs
  - output byte streams (VT/ANSI) and/or patches
  - input events (keyboard/mouse/touch/IME)
  - resize events
  - tick/time deltas (explicitly injected)
- Checkpoints
  - frame hashes and/or patch hashes
  - optional grid snapshots for debugging
- Metadata
  - run_id, git_sha/build_id, seed
  - size, DPR (web), terminal profile/capabilities, TERM identity (remote)
  - perf counters: timing histograms, patch sizes, bytes in/out

Design Constraints
- Forward-migratable schema (versioned, with strict validation).
- Reducible/minimizable: traces must be amenable to automatic minimization (delta debugging).

Acceptance Criteria
- Trace format is versioned and forward-migratable.
- Can be produced by native E2E scripts, web harnesses, and remote PTY sessions.
- Validator produces actionable errors (line + field context).
","notes":"Drafted canonical trace spec at docs/spec/frankenterm-golden-trace-format.md (JSONL bundle layout, record types, hashing/normalization, payload sidecars, CI validation + minimization strategy).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMill","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:55:10.780434801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:45:08.703034522Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:45:08.703008854Z","close_reason":"Completed: docs/spec/frankenterm-golden-trace-format.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:55:10.780434801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:55:10.780434801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.12","title":"CI: run golden trace replay gates (core + web + remote)","description":"Goal
- Turn the golden corpus into a hard regression gate.

Scope
- Replay traces through:
  - frankenterm-core (parser/grid)
  - ftui-web runner (state machine + rendering)
  - remote bridge loop (where feasible)

Acceptance Criteria
- CI fails on checksum mismatches with actionable diffs (frame/event indices).
- Time-bounded execution for PRs; expanded coverage on nightly.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.13","title":"Alien Artifact: failing-trace minimizer (delta debugging + VOI)","description":"Goal
- When a golden trace fails (checksum mismatch), automatically minimize it to the smallest repro trace that still fails.

Why (User Value)
- Makes regressions debuggable in minutes: a minimized trace is actionable and can be attached to issues/PRs.

Techniques (Alien Artifact)
- Start with classic delta debugging (ddmin) on the event stream.
- Upgrade with Value-of-Information (VOI) heuristics to pick the next reduction step that maximizes expected information gain per replay cost.
- Maintain an evidence ledger: each reduction attempt logs (candidate size, replay time, pass/fail, inferred minimality bounds).

Deliverables
- CLI/API to minimize a trace given a predicate ("replay mismatches").
- Produces a new trace file + a minimization report (JSON/JSONL).

Acceptance Criteria
- On a representative failing trace, produces >=10x size reduction while preserving the failure.
- Deterministic: repeated minimization yields identical minimized trace on same build.
- Integrates into CI failure artifacts (minimized trace attached when feasible/time-bounded).
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.13","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.13","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.13","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.14","title":"Alien Artifact: exhaustive small-state model checking for terminal invariants","description":"Goal
- Go beyond randomized property tests: exhaustively explore small terminal states to prove invariants hold under all short sequences of operations.

Approach
- Enumerate state space for tiny grids (e.g. 2x2, 3x3) and bounded scrollback.
- Enumerate short operation sequences (parser ops, resize ops, selection ops) and verify invariants after each step.
- Use pruning + canonicalization to keep exploration tractable.

Deliverables
- A deterministic model-check runner producing a coverage report (state counts, transition counts, found counterexamples).
- Any counterexample becomes a regression fixture in the conformance corpus and/or trace corpus.

Acceptance Criteria
- Runs time-bounded in CI (small sizes) and extended locally (bigger bounds).
- Finds at least one non-trivial bug during development (expectation: state machines are hard).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.6","title":"Alien Artifact: formal cost models for caches + scheduling (renderer + parser)","description":"Goal
- Use principled math to design cache eviction, batching, and scheduling with explicit objective functions.

Examples
- Explicit loss functions for cache misses vs memory.
- Queueing/scheduling model for input vs render vs present.
- Evidence ledger: why a policy was chosen, with measured priors/posteriors.

Acceptance Criteria
- Documented models + experiments that justify the final policies.
- Results integrated into code comments and perf docs.
